questionText: Are greenhouse gas emissions and/or water-related impacts from the production, processing/manufacturing, distribution activities or the consumption of your products relevant to your current CDP disclosure?
questionKo: Are 온실가스 배출량 및/또는 water-related impacts from the production, 프로세스ing/manufacturing, distribution activities 또는 the consumption of your products relevant to your current CDP disclosure?
sectorTags: Agricultural commodities, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry
simpleHelp: Business activities within the ‘agricultural commodities', ‘food, beverage and tobacco', and ‘paper and forestry' sectors are directly related to greenhouse gas emissions and water usage. Disclosing the activities that these organizations engage in - whether in direct operations or in other parts of the value chain -provides data users with the contextual information necessary to understand and evaluate how they assess and manage climate- and water-related dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities. This also enables data users to make more accurate cross-organizational and cross-industry comparisons.

columns.text: Relevance of emissions and/or water-related impacts
options: Both direct operations and upstream/downstream value chain
columns.textKo: Relevance of emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts
optionsKo: Both 직접 운영사업장 및 upstream/다운스트림 가치사슬

columns.text: Primary reason emissions and/or water-related impacts from this activity are not relevant
options: Lack of internal resources, capabilities, or expertise (e.g., due to organization size)
Judged to be unimportant or not relevant
Not evaluated due to insufficient data on operations
Outside the value chain of my organization
columns.textKo: 주된 이유 emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts from this activity are not 관련
optionsKo: 부족 internal resources, capabilities, 또는 expertise (e.g., due to organization size)
Judged to be unimportant 또는 not 관련
Not evaluated due to insufficient data on operations
Outside the 가치사슬 of my organization

columns.text: Explain why emissions and/or water-related impacts from this activity are not relevant
columns.textKo: 그 이유를 설명 emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts from this activity are not 관련

guidance.text: Your response to this question determines which questions will be shown throughout the questionnaire and which response options will be listed within these questions. If your response to this question is amended, data in those dependent questions may be erased.
guidance.textKo: Your response to this 문항 determines which 문항 will be shown throughout the 질문서 및 which response options will be listed within these 문항. 만약 your response to this 문항 is amended, data in those dependent 문항 may be erased.

일반 Consider aspects associated with the listed activities that are 관련 to the 농산물 원자재, food, beverage, 및 tobacco, 및/또는 paper 및 forestry sectors, e.g. the 프로세스ing/manufacturing of soft commodities 또는 food, beverage, tobacco 및/또는 wood-based goods. Activity (열 0) Respond to each specified activity, considering the entire 가치사슬 of 귀사. Emissions sources 및 water-related impacts of production activities: Carbon dioxide, methane, 및 nitrous oxide emissions released as a result of decaying 또는 burning of biological 또는 soil organic matter, fuel combustion 및 electricity generation in farm operation, digestion of livestock, stored manures 및 rice cultivation. Contribution to water stress regarding surface 및/또는 groundwater resources through excessive 또는 unsustainable water 관리 practices. Alteration of water availability due to land use changes (e.g., deforestation, irrigation practices). Pollution of water sources through runoff, 포함 pesticides, fertilizers 및 other chemicals. Emissions 및 water-related impacts 의 프로세스ing of raw materials 및 wood 또는 the manufacture of food, beverage 및 tobacco products are associated with all 프로세스es adopted, 및 all methods 및 techniques used, to transform raw agricultural inputs/timber products into final products ready for human consumption, 포함: Pre-프로세스ing (관련 만약 companies use 프로세스ed inputs, e.g. sugar); Primary grading/ screening to ensure uniformity; Storage during different 프로세스ing stages; Cleaning to remove 및 separate off-specification material, organic 및 non-organic debris, metals, 및 pesticide residues among other contaminants; Cutting, trimming, rolling 및 peeling to re-shape 및 remove inedible parts; Cooking, canning, evaporating, drying 및 freezing; Pulping 및 filtration; Packaging 의 final product to provide containment, protection, communication, 및 convenience, 포함, packaging for transport e.g. crates 또는 pallets; 및 Waste generated during 프로세스ing activities. Distribution encompasses the entire network required to move products through the 가치사슬 from the farm/production unit to the retail location. Consider all the distribution stages in your 공시: Transportation of raw agricultural/forest products to 프로세스ing facilities; Transportation of material inputs to 프로세스ing facilities, for example, packaging materials, chemicals, wood 및 하나 이상 other ingredients; Product distribution from 프로세스ing facilities to the retailer/customer; Transportation of waste to disposal sites 또는 to 점 of re-use; In each 의 cases listed for transportation above, you 해야 합니다 also account for emissions 및 water-related impacts from empty return journeys, storage of goods during distribution (as this can often require specific controls for humidity, temperature, atmospheric conditions 및 hygiene requirements) 및 the waste generated during transportation. The consumption stage includes the use of goods as well as their waste 관리, 포함 disposal 및 end-of-life treatment of products sold by the reporting organization. Consider: Cooling, freezing 및 heating of sold products; Waste disposal 및 end of life treatment of products, i.e., activities associated with land filling, incineration, composting, repurposing, recycling. Discharge treatment Note that the 계산 of emissions 및 water-related impacts associated with consumption 및 end life treatment may require reporting companies to make assumptions regarding how consumers use products, product lifetimes 및 end of life treatment methods chosen by consumers. Relevance of emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts (열 1) 만약 귀사 has emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts from an activity, specify which aspects of your 가치사슬 it relates to. Select: "Own land only" 만약 all your agricultural products are g행n on self-owned/managed farms; "가치사슬 (제외 own land)" 만약 all your agricultural products are g행n on farms which are not owned/managed by 귀사; "가치사슬 (포함 own land)" 만약 your agricultural products are g행n on self-owned/managed farms, 및 on farms not owned/managed by 귀사; "Upstream/다운스트림 가치사슬 (제외 직접 운영사업장)" 만약 all your agricultural inputs are purchased; 또는 "Both 직접 운영사업장 및 upstream/다운스트림 가치사슬" 만약 a combination of product cultivation 및 purchasing is 해당되는 경우 to 귀사. 만약 you wish to add 또는 delete an activity later in the 질문서, revisit this 문항 및 modify your response accordingly. Selecting "아니요" will erase your previous responses to linked 문항. 주된 이유 emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts from this activity are not 관련 (열 2) 만약 "아니요" is 선택 in 열 1 "Relevance of emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts", select the 주된 이유 why emissions 및/또는 water related impacts are not 관련 to your CDP 공시. 만약 either "가치사슬 (제외 own land)" 또는 "Upstream/다운스트림 가치사슬 (제외 직접 운영사업장)" is 선택 in 열 1 "Relevance of emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts", select the 주된 이유 why emissions 및/또는 water related impacts are not 관련 to your own land 또는 직접 운영사업장. 그 이유를 설명 emissions 및/또는 water-related impacts from this activity are not 관련 (열 3) 만약 "Analysis in progress", "Do not own/manage land", "Outside the 가치사슬 of my organization" 또는 "Judged to be unimportant 또는 not 관련" is 선택 in 열 2 "주된 이유 for no emissions 또는 water-related impacts from this activity", 설명 your evaluation methods, indicating the 절차s 및 tools used for evaluating the relevance of this activity. Specify parts of your business included in the analysis 및 the 기준 used to decide that the activity was not 관련. 만약 "Analysis in progress" is 선택 in 열 2 "주된 이유 for no emissions 또는 water-related impacts from this activity", provide a date for 경우 it will be finalized. 만약 "부족 internal resources, capabilities, 또는 expertise (e.g., due to organization size)" is 선택 in 열 2 "주된 이유 for no emissions 또는 water-related impacts from this activity", specify the main challenges you experience. 만약 "부족 internal resources, capabilities, 또는 expertise (e.g., due to organization size)" 또는 "Not evaluated due to insufficient data on operations" is 선택 in 열 2 "주된 이유 for no emissions 또는 water-related impacts from this activity", indicate 만약 you have 하나 이상 plans to evaluate the relevancy of this activity to your 공시 in the next two years 및 만약 so, 설명 the methods 및 coverage for this upcoming analysis.

guidance.text:
Consumption: the use of goods, waste disposal and end-of-life treatment of products sold by the reporting organization. 
Distribution: the entire network required to move products through the value chain from the production unit (e.g., farm/forest) to the retail location. 
Manufacturing : the series of actions, methods, and techniques that transform raw or processed materials into final products ready for human use/consumption. 
Processing: the series of actions that transform raw materials from their natural state to an initial processed state in preparation for market or further processing/manufacturing. 
Production: the first stage of the upstream value chain, often entailing the production of raw agricultural and forest products by farm owners, smallholders, and communities. Vertically integrated companies are also involved in producing activities if they own or manage land used for production (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 and AFi, 2024 ). 
Value chain: the entire sequence of upstream and downstream activities, sites, resources, and relationships associated with the reporting organization's operations, starting with the raw materials and extending through end-of-life management, aimed at providing or receiving value from an organization's products and services within, upstream, or downstream of direct operations (adapted from GHG, 2013 ; ESRS, 2023 ; SBTN, 2023 ). 
Water availability : the natural runoff (through groundwater and rivers) minus the flow of water that is required to sustain freshwater and estuarine ecosystems and the human livelihoods and well-being that depend on these ecosystems (adapted from CEO Water Mandate's "Corporate Water Disclosure Guidelines" , 2014). 
Water stress (‘areas with'): a concept that considers physical quantity aspects related to water resources, including water availability. As good practice, a water stressed area should be measured at the catchment level as a minimum. Commonly accepted global indicators to assess areas as water stressed and their thresholds for reporting to CDP include: 
Water availability - risk category equal to/greater than ‘High risk': 3.4 ( WWF Water Risk Filter) . WWF recommends that users also take into consideration ‘Medium risk': 2.6. This category is based on a multi-model approach which integrates the best available global water scarcity risk indicators: water depletion, baseline water stress, freshwater quantity target and groundwater availability. In addition, WWF recommends that users take into consideration locations facing 'Drought' risk as these can exacerbate risks related to 'Water Availability'.
Baseline water stress - indicator equal to/greater than ‘High': 40-80% ( WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas) . This refers to ratio of total annual water withdrawals to available renewable water supply. 
Baseline water depletion - indicator equal to/greater than ‘High': 50-75% ( WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas) . This refers to the ratio of total annual water consumption to available renewable water supply.
guidance.textKo: Consumption: the use of goods, waste disposal 및 end-of-life treatment of products sold by the reporting organization. Distribution: the entire network required to move products through the 가치사슬 from the production unit (e.g., farm/forest) to the retail location. Manufacturing: the series of actions, methods, 및 techniques that transform raw 또는 프로세스ed materials into final products ready for human use/consumption. 프로세스ing: the series of actions that transform raw materials from their natural state to an initial 프로세스ed state in preparation for market 또는 further 프로세스ing/manufacturing. Production: the first stage 의 업스트림 가치사슬, often entailing the production of raw agricultural 및 forest products by farm owners, smallholders, 및 communities. Vertically integrated companies are also involved in producing activities 만약 they own 또는 manage land used for production (adapted from Global Canopy, 2018 및 AFi, 2024). 가치사슬: the entire sequence of upstream 및 downstream activities, sites, resources, 및 relationships associated with the reporting 조직의 operations, starting with the raw materials 및 extending through end-of-life 관리, aimed at providing 또는 receiving value from an 조직의 제품 및 서비스 within, upstream, 또는 downstream of 직접 운영사업장 (adapted from GHG, 2013; ESRS, 2023; SBTN, 2023). Water availability: the natural runoff (through groundwater 및 rivers) minus the flow of water that is required to sustain freshwater 및 estuarine ecosystems 및 the human livelihoods 및 well-being that depend on these ecosystems (adapted from CEO Water Mandate's "Corporate Water 공시 Guidelines", 2014). Water stress (‘areas with'): a concept that considers physical quantity aspects related to water resources, 포함 water availability. As good practice, a water stressed area 해야 합니다 be measured at the catchment level as a 최소. Commonly accepted global indicators to assess areas as water stressed 및 their thresholds for reporting to CDP include: Water availability - risk category equal to/초과 ‘High risk': 3.4 (WWF Water Risk Filter). WWF recommends that users also take into consideration ‘Medium risk': 2.6. This category is based on a multi-model approach which integrates the best available global water scarcity risk indicators: water depletion, baseline water stress, freshwater quantity 목표 및 groundwater availability. In addition, WWF recommends that users take into consideration locations facing 'Drought' risk as these can exacerbate 리스크 related to 'Water Availability'. Baseline water stress - indicator equal to/초과 ‘High': 40-80% (WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas). This refers to ratio of total annual water withdrawals to available re신규able water supply. Baseline water depletion - indicator equal to/초과 ‘High': 50-75% (WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas). This refers to the ratio of total annual water consumption to available re신규able water supply.

sectorTagsKo: 농산물 원자재, Food, beverage & tobacco, 제지 및 임업
simpleHelpKo: Business activities within the ‘농산물 원자재', ‘food, beverage 및 tobacco', 및 ‘paper 및 forestry' sectors are directly related to 온실가스 배출량 및 water usage. Disclosing the activities that these organizations engage in - whether in 직접 운영사업장 또는 in other parts 의 가치사슬 -provides 데이터 사용자 with the contextual information necessary to understand 및 evaluate how they assess 및 manage climate- 및 water-related dependencies, impacts, 리스크, 및 기회. This also enables 데이터 사용자 to make more accurate cross-organizational 및 cross-industry comparisons.
